title: Add E2E test for Keystone upgrade from 2025.2 to 2026.1
description:
# GitHub Issue #157: Add E2E test for Keystone release upgrade from 2025.2 to 2026.1

**Category**: testing | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Add a Chainsaw E2E test `tests/e2e/keystone/release-upgrade/` that validates a full Keystone upgrade from OpenStack 2025.2 to 2026.1, including database schema migration. This test depends on the expand-migrate-contract migration strategy being implemented in the operator (see #155).

The test should:

1. Deploy Keystone CR with image tag `2025.2`, wait for `Ready=True`
2. Verify the API is accessible at `/v3`
3. Patch `spec.image.tag` to `2026.1`
4. Assert that the expand/migrate phase completes (new status condition or event)
5. Assert that the Deployment rolls to the 2026.1 image
6. Assert that the contract phase completes
7. Assert `Ready=True` after the full upgrade
8. Verify the API is still accessible at `/v3` with the new version

Both 2025.2 and 2026.1 images are already built and loaded into the kind cluster by CI — no CI workflow changes are required.

**Motivation**: The existing `image-upgrade` test only validates a tag swap within the same release (2025.2 → 2025.2-upgraded) without any database migration. A cross-release upgrade (2025.2 → 2026.1) involves Keystone schema changes (28.0.0 → 29.0.0) that exercise the full expand-migrate-contract path. Without this test, there is no CI validation that the operator can safely upgrade Keystone across OpenStack releases.

**Affected Areas**:
- `tests/e2e/keystone/release-upgrade/chainsaw-test.yaml` (new)
- `tests/e2e/keystone/release-upgrade/00-keystone-cr.yaml` (new)
- `tests/e2e/keystone/release-upgrade/01-patch-upgrade.yaml` (new)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] E2E test deploys Keystone 2025.2, upgrades to 2026.1, and validates full lifecycle
- [ ] All three migration phases (expand+migrate, rolling update, contract) are verified
- [ ] API remains accessible after the upgrade
- [ ] `Ready=True` is reached after the full upgrade completes
- [ ] Existing E2E tests (`basic-deployment`, `basic-deployment-2026-1`, `image-upgrade`) continue to pass

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/157

stories.title: CI validates cross-release Keystone upgrade lifecycle
stories.want: an E2E test that deploys Keystone 2025.2, upgrades to 2026.1 via the expand-migrate-contract path, and verifies the full lifecycle completes
stories.so_that: database schema migration regressions (keystone 28.0.0 → 29.0.0) are caught before merging
criteria: Chainsaw test deploys Keystone CR with image tag 2025.2 and reaches Ready=True with installedRelease=2025.2
Test patches spec.image.tag to 2026.1 and asserts Ready=True with installedRelease=2026.1 within 5m
Test verifies expand, migrate, and contract Jobs (keystone-release-upgrade-db-{expand,migrate,contract}) were created
Test runs in parallel with existing suites using unique CR name keystone-release-upgrade and database keystone_release_upgrade

stories.title: Operator verifies API continuity during release upgrade
stories.want: confirmation that the Keystone API at /v3 remains accessible both before and after a cross-release upgrade
stories.so_that: I can trust the operator to perform zero-downtime upgrades across OpenStack releases
criteria: GET /v3 returns a successful response before the upgrade (Keystone 2025.2 serving)
GET /v3 returns a successful response after the upgrade completes (Keystone 2026.1 serving)
API accessibility is tested via kubectl run with a python3 urllib.request call to the Keystone Service ClusterIP
Both curl tests use unique pod names (curl-test-release-pre, curl-test-release-post) to avoid collisions

stories.title: Operator verifies Deployment rolls to new image during upgrade
stories.want: verification that the Deployment container image updates to 2026.1 and all pods complete the rollout
stories.so_that: I know both the database schema and the running code are on the target release
criteria: Deployment keystone-release-upgrade-api container image ends with 2026.1 after upgrade
Deployment status shows updatedReplicas == replicas (all pods rolled)
Deployment status shows availableReplicas > 0 (at least one pod serving)

stories.title: Developer understands release-upgrade test via reference documentation
stories.want: the release-upgrade test suite documented in the existing keystone-e2e-tests.md reference page
stories.so_that: I can understand what it validates, how it differs from image-upgrade and upgrade-flow, and troubleshoot failures
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md includes a release-upgrade section with purpose, steps table, and fixtures list
Test suite inventory table includes release-upgrade row with CR name and requirements
Overview diagram and file layout section updated to include release-upgrade
Documentation follows existing section format (Purpose, Steps table, Fixtures)

stories.title: Release-upgrade test runs without interfering with existing tests
stories.want: the release-upgrade test to use isolated resources that do not conflict with basic-deployment, basic-deployment-2026-1, image-upgrade, or upgrade-flow tests
stories.so_that: all E2E tests pass when run in parallel
criteria: CR name keystone-release-upgrade is unique across all test suites
Database name keystone_release_upgrade is unique across all test suites
Curl test pod names are unique (curl-test-release-pre, curl-test-release-post)
No CI workflow changes required — 2025.2 and 2026.1 images already loaded into kind

requirements.description: The test SHALL deploy a Keystone CR with image tag 2025.2 and assert Ready=True with installedRelease=2025.2
requirements.rationale: Establishes the baseline deployment state before triggering the cross-release upgrade

scenarios.name: Initial deployment reaches Ready
scenarios.when: Keystone CR with spec.image.tag=2025.2 is applied to the openstack namespace
scenarios.then: status.conditions[?type=='Ready'] has status=True and reason=AllReady within 5 minutes

scenarios.name: Initial Deployment image is correct
scenarios.then: Deployment keystone-release-upgrade-api container image ends with 2025.2

scenarios.name: All sub-conditions reach True
scenarios.then: SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, and BootstrapReady all have status=True

requirements.description: The test SHALL verify the Keystone API is accessible at /v3 before the upgrade
requirements.rationale: Confirms the baseline deployment is serving traffic before triggering the upgrade

scenarios.name: Pre-upgrade API responds
scenarios.when: Keystone 2025.2 is Ready and a curl pod sends GET to http://keystone-release-upgrade-api.openstack.svc:5000/v3
scenarios.then: the request returns successfully with a JSON response body

scenarios.name: Curl test pod completes
scenarios.when: kubectl run curl-test-release-pre executes the python3 urllib request
scenarios.then: the pod exits with status 0
and_then: pod is cleaned up via --rm flag

scenarios.name: API is unreachable if Keystone not Ready
scenarios.when: the test step runs after the Ready=True assertion
scenarios.then: the API is guaranteed to be accessible because the assertion already passed

requirements.description: The test SHALL patch spec.image.tag to 2026.1 to trigger the expand-migrate-contract upgrade
requirements.rationale: The cross-release tag change (2025.2 → 2026.1) triggers the phased migration path, exercising keystone schema 28.0.0 → 29.0.0

scenarios.name: Patch triggers upgrade
scenarios.when: spec.image.tag is patched from 2025.2 to 2026.1 via merge-patch
scenarios.then: the operator detects a sequential upgrade and initiates the expand-migrate-contract state machine
and_then: status.upgradePhase transitions through Expanding, Migrating, RollingUpdate, Contracting

scenarios.name: Patch file targets correct CR
scenarios.then: only the spec.image.tag field is modified on Keystone CR keystone-release-upgrade

scenarios.name: Patch uses correct apiVersion and kind
scenarios.when: 01-patch-upgrade.yaml is applied via Chainsaw patch step
scenarios.then: the patch targets apiVersion keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1 kind Keystone

requirements.description: The test SHALL verify that all three migration phase Jobs (expand, migrate, contract) are created during the upgrade
requirements.rationale: Proves the operator used the phased migration path rather than a simple db_sync, which is the core differentiator from the image-upgrade test

scenarios.name: Expand Job exists
scenarios.then: Job keystone-release-upgrade-db-expand exists in the openstack namespace

scenarios.name: Migrate Job exists
scenarios.then: Job keystone-release-upgrade-db-migrate exists in the openstack namespace

scenarios.name: Contract Job exists
scenarios.then: Job keystone-release-upgrade-db-contract exists in the openstack namespace

requirements.description: The test SHALL verify the Deployment rolls to the 2026.1 image with all pods updated
requirements.rationale: Confirms the RollingUpdate phase completed and the running API servers are on the new release

scenarios.name: Container image updated
scenarios.then: Deployment keystone-release-upgrade-api container[0] image ends with 2026.1

scenarios.name: All pods rolled
scenarios.then: Deployment status shows updatedReplicas == replicas

scenarios.name: No old pods remain
scenarios.then: all running pods use the 2026.1 image

requirements.description: The test SHALL assert Ready=True with installedRelease=2026.1 after the full upgrade completes
requirements.rationale: Confirms the end state of the upgrade — the operator considers the upgrade fully complete and the CR is healthy

scenarios.name: Ready condition True after upgrade
scenarios.when: all upgrade phases complete (expand, migrate, rolling update, contract)
scenarios.then: status.conditions[?type=='Ready'] has status=True and reason=AllReady

scenarios.name: installedRelease updated
scenarios.then: status.installedRelease equals 2026.1

scenarios.name: Upgrade state cleared
scenarios.then: status.upgradePhase is empty and status.targetRelease is empty

requirements.description: The test SHALL verify the Keystone API is accessible at /v3 after the upgrade
requirements.rationale: Confirms the upgraded Keystone is serving traffic on the new release, completing the end-to-end lifecycle validation

scenarios.name: Post-upgrade API responds
scenarios.when: Keystone 2026.1 is Ready and a curl pod sends GET to http://keystone-release-upgrade-api.openstack.svc:5000/v3
scenarios.then: the request returns successfully with a JSON response body

scenarios.name: Post-upgrade curl pod completes
scenarios.when: kubectl run curl-test-release-post executes the python3 urllib request
scenarios.then: the pod exits with status 0
and_then: pod is cleaned up via --rm flag

scenarios.name: API endpoint unchanged after upgrade
scenarios.then: the Service URL and port (keystone-release-upgrade-api:5000) remain the same as before the upgrade

requirements.description: The test SHALL follow project Chainsaw conventions: openstack namespace, 5m assert timeout, diagnostic catch blocks
requirements.rationale: Consistency with existing 18 test suites ensures maintainability and familiar debugging experience

scenarios.name: Standard namespace and timeout
scenarios.then: namespace is openstack and timeouts.assert is 5m

scenarios.name: Diagnostic catch blocks on assertion steps
scenarios.then: the catch block captures pod logs (current + previous), job logs, pod descriptions, job status, and events
and_then: output follows the standard diagnostic script template used by all other tests

scenarios.name: SPDX license header present
scenarios.when: any YAML file in the test suite is created
scenarios.then: the file begins with the SPDX-FileCopyrightText and SPDX-License-Identifier comments

requirements.description: The test SHALL use unique resource names that do not collide with any existing E2E test suite
requirements.rationale: Chainsaw runs up to 4 tests in parallel; shared resource names would cause intermittent failures

scenarios.name: Unique CR and database names
scenarios.when: the test creates Keystone CR keystone-release-upgrade with database keystone_release_upgrade
scenarios.then: no other test suite uses either of these names
and_then: verified against: keystone-basic, keystone-upgrade, keystone-upgrade-flow, keystone-basic-2026-1, and all other CR names

scenarios.name: Unique curl test pod names
scenarios.when: the test creates kubectl run pods curl-test-release-pre and curl-test-release-post
scenarios.then: no other test suite uses these pod names
and_then: verified against: curl-test-basic used by basic-deployment

scenarios.name: No shared mutable state
scenarios.when: the release-upgrade test runs in parallel with other suites
scenarios.then: each test creates and manages only its own Keystone CR, database, and Jobs

tasks.title: Create test fixture files: 00-keystone-cr.yaml and 01-patch-upgrade.yaml (CC-0060, REQ-001, REQ-003, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create the tests/e2e/keystone/release-upgrade/ directory with two fixture files. 00-keystone-cr.yaml: Keystone CR named keystone-release-upgrade, replicas=1, image tag 2025.2, managed mode with clusterRef openstack-db, database keystone_release_upgrade, secretRef keystone-db, cache clusterRef openstack-memcached, fernet weekly rotation, bootstrap with keystone-admin. Follow the exact pattern from tests/e2e/keystone/upgrade-flow/00-keystone-cr.yaml. 01-patch-upgrade.yaml: merge-patch targeting keystone-release-upgrade, sets spec.image.tag to 2026.1. Follow the pattern from tests/e2e/keystone/upgrade-flow/01-patch-upgrade.yaml. Both files must include SPDX license headers with CC-0060 feature reference in comments.

tasks.title: Create chainsaw-test.yaml with full upgrade lifecycle validation (CC-0060, REQ-001 through REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create tests/e2e/keystone/release-upgrade/chainsaw-test.yaml with 6 steps: (1) Apply 00-keystone-cr.yaml. (2) Assert Ready=True + installedRelease=2025.2, verify Deployment image ends with 2025.2 via script, with diagnostic catch block. (3) Verify API at /v3 via kubectl run curl-test-release-pre using ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:2025.2 image and python3 urllib.request. (4) Patch via 01-patch-upgrade.yaml. (5) Assert Ready=True + installedRelease=2026.1, verify expand/migrate/contract Jobs exist via script, assert Deployment image ends with 2026.1 + updatedReplicas==replicas + availableReplicas>0, with diagnostic catch block. (6) Verify API at /v3 via kubectl run curl-test-release-post using ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:2026.1 image. Test name: keystone-release-upgrade, namespace: openstack, timeouts.assert: 5m. Follow patterns from upgrade-flow/chainsaw-test.yaml (assert + job verification), basic-deployment/chainsaw-test.yaml (curl test), and image-upgrade/chainsaw-test.yaml (deployment image check). Include SPDX header and CC-0060 feature reference.

tasks.title: Update keystone-e2e-tests.md reference documentation with release-upgrade suite (CC-0060, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Update docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md: (1) Add release-upgrade row to the test suite inventory table (CR name: keystone-release-upgrade, behavior: cross-release upgrade 2025.2→2026.1 with expand-migrate-contract migration, requirements: feature-specific). (2) Add a release-upgrade section under Test Suite Details following the existing format (Purpose, Steps table with #/Step Name/Type/Details columns, Fixtures, Design note explaining how this differs from image-upgrade and upgrade-flow). (3) Update the Overview section test count (from 9 to 10 or current count + 1). (4) Add release-upgrade to the ASCII diagram and file layout section. Update all counts and references consistently.

review_criteria: All 9 SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-009) have corresponding test steps in chainsaw-test.yaml
Test follows existing Chainsaw patterns: namespace openstack, 5m assert timeout, diagnostic catch blocks matching the template from upgrade-flow and basic-deployment
All fixture files include SPDX-FileCopyrightText license headers and CC-0060 feature reference in comments
CR name (keystone-release-upgrade), database name (keystone_release_upgrade), and curl pod names are unique across all 18+ existing test suites
Upgrade phase Jobs (expand, migrate, contract) are verified via script step, proving the phased migration path was used rather than simple db_sync
API accessibility verified both before upgrade (2025.2) and after upgrade (2026.1) using the kubectl run curl pattern from basic-deployment
Deployment image assertion confirms container image ends with 2026.1 and updatedReplicas==replicas post-upgrade
docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md updated with release-upgrade section including purpose, steps table, fixtures, and design note
No CI workflow changes introduced — test relies on existing image loading (2025.2 and 2026.1 already loaded into kind by ci.yaml)
implementation_notes:
This is a testing-only feature: 3 new YAML files + 1 docs update. No Go code, no API changes, no frontend.

**Architecture:** The release-upgrade test is a Chainsaw E2E test that exercises the expand-migrate-contract upgrade path implemented in CC-0056. It differs from the existing upgrade-flow test (CC-0056) by focusing on the user-facing lifecycle (API accessibility before/after) rather than internal state machine mechanics (skip-level rejection). It differs from image-upgrade (CC-0016) by triggering a cross-release upgrade (2025.2→2026.1) that requires database schema migration (keystone 28.0.0→29.0.0) rather than a same-release tag swap (2025.2→2025.2-upgraded).

**Data Flow:**
```
Apply CR (tag=2025.2) → operator reconciles → db_sync → Ready=True
  → curl /v3 (verify API)
  → patch tag to 2026.1
  → operator detects upgrade → Expanding (db_sync --expand) → Migrating (db_sync --migrate)
  → RollingUpdate (Deployment rolls pods) → Contracting (db_sync --contract)
  → installedRelease=2026.1, Ready=True
  → curl /v3 (verify API still works)
```

**Key Files to Follow:**
- tests/e2e/keystone/upgrade-flow/chainsaw-test.yaml — primary pattern for upgrade assertions and Job verification
- tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment/chainsaw-test.yaml — pattern for API curl tests
- tests/e2e/keystone/image-upgrade/chainsaw-test.yaml — pattern for Deployment image verification

**Pitfalls:**
- The curl test pod names must be unique per test suite to avoid conflicts during parallel execution.
- The 5m assert timeout must be sufficient for the full expand→migrate→rolling-update→contract cycle; the upgrade-flow test uses the same timeout successfully.
- The Deployment image check (grep for 2026.1) should match the end of the image string to avoid false positives from the repository path.

**Fullstack Checklist:** Not applicable — this is a pure E2E test feature with no backend, frontend, or API changes.
